medical_history.history_of_present_illness: Ms. ___ is a ___ female with a remote history of alcohol use disorder, pancreatitis, who presents with abdominal pain. Patient reports that this episode of abdominal pain started around 5 days prior to admission. It felt like a 'nervous stomach', which she describes 'like a fist in the stomach' with tightness and pulling located in the middle of her stomach. She thought this was related to stress, but it continued to get worse and has been severe at times. Associated with nausea, but no vomiting, and generally a poor appetite. She notes that she has had hard stool, but that her pain did not improve after a bowel movement. She states that she will often have one bowel movement a week. On review of records, patient has been seen in GI clinic at ___. She underwent an upper endoscopy in ___ which showed gastric polyps. She was started on esomeprazole twice daily, which she continues to take.
